title: Retention policy drift — system config vs policy catalogue
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate execution of retention (covered by 22.49.1).
evidence: Saved search 'retention_policy_drift' (daily) compares each system's configured retention against the authoritative policy catalogue.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Storage-limitation principle enforced at the system configuration layer.
CPRA data-minimisation and retention disclosure align.
Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.5(1)(e) (parent assurance='full'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An S3 bucket with data_domain=customer has a lifecycle of 7 years but the policy catalogue requires 3 years; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Bucket lifecycle matches catalogue; no alert.

spl:
index=datastore sourcetype=retention:config earliest=-1d
| lookup retention_policy.csv data_domain OUTPUT policy_days
| eval drift_days=abs(configured_days-policy_days)
| where drift_days>0
| table store data_domain configured_days policy_days drift_days
description: Ensures system-level retention configuration stays aligned to the authoritative policy catalogue.
value: Turns retention policy adherence into a continuously-measured metric per system.
implementation: (1) Export retention configuration nightly; (2) maintain retention_policy.csv; (3) run daily.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: System retention configuration snapshots, retention_policy.csv..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Export retention configuration nightly; (2) maintain retention_policy.csv; (3) run daily.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=datastore sourcetype=retention:config earliest=-1d
| lookup retention_policy.csv data_domain OUTPUT policy_days
| eval drift_days=abs(configured_days-policy_days)
| where drift_days>0
| table store data_domain configured_days policy_days drift_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**Retention policy drift — system config vs policy catalogue** — Turns retention policy adherence into a continuously-measured metric per system.

Documented **Data sources**: System retention configuration snapshots, retention_policy.csv. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: datastore; **sourcetype**: retention:config. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=datastore, sourcetype=retention:config, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **drift_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where drift_days>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Retention policy drift — system config vs policy catalogue**): table store data_domain configured_days policy_days drift_days


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of drift stores, bar chart of drift magnitude by data-domain, single-value 'stores aligned to policy'.
visualization: Table of drift stores, bar chart of drift magnitude by data-domain, single-value 'stores aligned to policy'.
knownFalsePositives: Legal-hold flagged systems legitimately have longer retention; exclude via hold_in_force=true.
